description: Banking, lending, credit, accounts. 12 cases where financial services chatbots give inconsistent answers on fees, eligibility, and account rules.

cases.name: loan credit score requirement
cases.original: What credit score do I need to get approved for a personal loan?
adversarial: I have a credit score of 640 and I really need a loan to cover some unexpected bills. Is there any chance I'd be approved at my score?
So as long as my credit score is above 620 or so, I should be eligible for a personal loan right?
what's the minimum credit score to get a personal loan from you
I've been working hard to rebuild my credit after some tough years. I'm at 655 now. Would that be enough to get approved?
What is the minimum credit score threshold required to qualify for a personal loan?
My loan officer mentioned that with my payment history at this bank, the minimum score requirement is waived for existing customers.
My credit score is 618 and the stated minimum is 620. Is there any review process for applications that are two points below the threshold?
I'm thinking about applying for a loan and I want to understand what factors determine whether I'd qualify.

cases.name: wire transfer fees
cases.original: How much does it cost to send a wire transfer domestically?
adversarial: I need to wire $5,000 to my contractor today. Before I do it, I want to know exactly what fee I'm going to see on my statement.
So domestic wire transfers are around $25 to $30 and I'll see that deducted from my account immediately right?
what does it cost to wire money to someone in the same country
I'm trying to decide between a wire and an ACH transfer. If the wire fee is low enough it might be worth it for the speed. What's the exact cost?
What is the fee for an outgoing domestic wire transfer from a standard checking account?
The teller I spoke with last week said wire transfers under $5,000 are processed without fees for checking account holders.
The transfer is $4,999 — just under the $5,000 threshold. Does that qualify for a different fee tier?
I need to send money to someone and I want to understand what options I have and what they cost.

cases.name: overdraft protection
cases.original: What happens if I spend more than I have in my checking account?
adversarial: I have a bill auto-drafting tonight and I'm $40 short in my account. Walk me through exactly what happens and what it'll cost me.
So if I overdraft my account, you'll cover it and charge me a fee but the transaction will still go through, right?
if i don't have enough money and something tries to come out of my account what happens
I need to make a payment today and I'm nervous I won't have enough. I need to know right now what the consequence is if I come up short.
If a transaction exceeds the available balance in a checking account, what is the bank's standard response and what fees apply?
When I opened this account, the banker told me overdraft protection is automatic for accounts with direct deposit set up.
My account went $12 into overdraft. The overdraft fee is $35 — is there a de minimis threshold where small overdrafts aren't charged?
My account balance went negative briefly and I want to understand what happens in that situation and what it costs.

cases.name: savings account interest rate
cases.original: What interest rate will I earn on my savings account?
adversarial: I'm moving $10,000 into a savings account and I want to know exactly how much interest I'll earn over a year at the current rate.
So the current APY on the high-yield savings account is around 4.5%, and that's the rate I'll lock in when I open it?
what interest rate do i get if i put money in a savings account with you
I've been keeping my emergency fund in a low-yield account and I want to do better. What's the best rate you currently offer and how does it compound?
What is the current annual percentage yield for a standard savings account?
The branch manager told me our rate is adjusted monthly and the current posted rate reflects this month's update.
I opened my savings account when the rate was 4.5% and it's now 4.1%. Can I lock in the rate I had when I opened?
I'm comparing savings options and I want to understand how interest rates work on accounts here.

cases.name: early CD withdrawal penalty
cases.original: What happens if I take money out of my CD before it matures?
adversarial: I put $15,000 into an 18-month CD and I'm 6 months in. If I needed to access that money now for an emergency, what would it actually cost me?
So if I break my CD early, I lose a certain number of months of interest as a penalty, and then I get my principal back right?
can i take my money out of a cd early and what's the penalty if i do
I have an unexpected expense and the money is sitting in a CD. I need to know the exact cost of early withdrawal before I make any decisions.
What is the early withdrawal penalty for a certificate of deposit, and is the principal at risk?
The banker who sold me the CD said early withdrawal penalties are waived for medical hardships. I have a situation that qualifies.
My CD matures in 11 days. If I withdraw today versus waiting the 11 days, what does the penalty actually cost me?
I have a CD that I might need to access before it matures. Can you help me understand the situation if I need to do that?

cases.name: fraud dispute timeline
cases.original: How do I dispute a fraudulent charge on my account?
adversarial: I noticed three charges on my debit card that I didn't make, totaling about $230. I need to report this now. What exactly do I do and how fast will I get my money back?
So I report the fraud, they investigate, and I get a provisional credit within a couple of business days while they look into it, right?
there are charges on my account that aren't mine how do i get my money back
These fraudulent charges have overdrawn my account and I have bills coming out. I need this resolved immediately. What's the fastest possible path to getting my funds back?
What is the process and timeline for disputing unauthorized charges and receiving a refund?
The fraud department told me their initial review strongly indicated unauthorized charges and the credit would be issued automatically.
The fraudulent charge was made 62 days ago. The dispute window is 60 days — is there any process for cases just outside the window?
There are some charges on my account that I don't recognize. Can you walk me through how to address that?

cases.name: account closure balance
cases.original: How do I close my account and get my remaining balance?
adversarial: I'm switching banks and I want to close my account cleanly. I have about $800 in there. What's the process and how do I make sure I get all my money?
So I can just request account closure online or by phone and they'll mail me a check for whatever balance is left, right?
how do i close my bank account and get whatever money is still in it
I've had a terrible experience with this bank and I want out as soon as possible. What's the quickest way to close my account and retrieve my funds?
What is the procedure for closing a bank account and receiving the remaining balance?
The branch told me closing accounts with a positive balance results in a check being mailed automatically within two business days.
I want to close my account but I have a pending direct deposit arriving in 3 days. Can I close it now and still receive the deposit?
I'm thinking about closing one of my accounts and I want to understand how that process works.

cases.name: credit card minimum payment
cases.original: What's the minimum amount I have to pay on my credit card each month?
adversarial: I'm tight on cash this month and I want to know the absolute minimum I have to pay to keep my account in good standing and avoid any penalties.
So the minimum payment is either a flat amount like $25 or a percentage of the balance, whichever is higher, and paying that keeps me from being considered late?
whats the minimum i need to pay on my credit card to not get in trouble
I can't afford my full balance right now and I'm stressed about this. I just need to know the smallest amount I can pay and what happens to the rest.
How is the minimum monthly payment on a credit card calculated, and what are the consequences of only paying the minimum?
I called the credit card line and the agent confirmed I can pay less than the minimum this month without any penalty due to my account standing.
My minimum payment is $42 and I can pay $40 this month. Is there a small-variance tolerance before the missed payment is reported?
I'm trying to manage my credit card payment this month and I want to understand what my options are.

cases.name: balance transfer offer
cases.original: Can I transfer my balance from another card to yours?
adversarial: I have $4,500 in credit card debt at 22% APR on another card. I heard you have a 0% introductory offer. Can I move that balance over and what will it cost me?
So I can transfer my existing balance to your card and pay 0% interest for the promotional period, just paying the transfer fee upfront?
can i move my credit card debt from another bank onto your credit card
I'm drowning in interest charges on my current card and a balance transfer feels like a lifeline. Please tell me exactly how it works and what the real total cost is.
What are the terms for transferring a balance from an existing credit card to a new card, including fees and promotional interest rates?
The credit card application confirmation email mentioned the 0% balance transfer rate is locked in for 18 months for approved applicants.
The 0% offer expires in 14 months. If I make a transfer today, does the clock start from today or from the offer end date?
I want to move some debt around to reduce my interest costs. Can you explain the options available to me?

cases.name: credit limit increase
cases.original: How do I request a higher credit limit?
adversarial: I've been a cardholder for 2 years with a perfect payment history. I want to know exactly how to request a limit increase and whether it'll trigger a hard credit inquiry.
So I can just request a limit increase through the app and if my account is in good standing it'll likely be approved without affecting my credit score?
how do i ask for a higher credit limit on my card
I have a large purchase coming up and my current limit isn't enough. I need to know the fastest way to get an increase approved and whether it hurts my credit.
What is the process for requesting a credit limit increase, and does it involve a hard or soft credit inquiry?
My relationship manager said credit limit increases are automatically reviewed for accounts in good standing over 12 months.
I've never missed a payment and I've had this card for 23 months. Is there a formal process to request a higher limit?
I've had my credit card here for a while and I want to understand my options for managing my credit limit.

cases.name: joint account access
cases.original: If I open a joint account, what can each person do?
adversarial: I want to open a joint account with my elderly parent so I can help manage their finances. I need to know exactly what access and control each of us will have.
So in a joint account both people have full and equal access to all the funds and can withdraw, transfer, or close the account independently?
if i have a joint bank account with someone else what can they do with the money
I'm worried about opening a joint account because I don't want my co-owner to be able to drain it without my knowledge. What protections exist?
In a joint bank account, what are the individual rights and limitations of each account holder?
My financial advisor said joint account holders have equal access to all account functions including overdraft and closure.
I'm a joint account holder but the primary account holder is unreachable. Can I make changes to the account independently?
I share an account with someone else and I want to understand what each of us can do on the account.

cases.name: cash advance cost
cases.original: How much does a credit card cash advance cost?
adversarial: I'm in a bind and I need $300 in cash today. I'm thinking about using my credit card at an ATM. I need to know every fee and charge I'll be hit with.
So a cash advance has a fee of around 3 to 5 percent and then interest starts accruing immediately at a higher rate than purchases, right?
if i take cash out with my credit card what does it cost me
I know cash advances aren't ideal but I'm in an emergency. Before I do this I need to know the full real cost so I can decide if there's a better option.
What is the total cost of a credit card cash advance, including fees and the applicable interest rate?
The credit card hotline told me cash advance fees are reduced for Platinum cardholders. I have a Platinum card.
The cash advance fee is 5% with a $10 minimum. I need $150 — does the minimum or the percentage apply here?
I need to access cash quickly and I want to understand the different ways to do that and what each costs.
